# Trove

> Personal Content OS — semantic search across GitHub repos, local files, screenshots, videos. Open source, local-first, MCP server for Claude Code.

Trove indexes everything a developer/creator produces and makes it searchable. It's a bridge: it knows where every file lives but never copies or hosts anything.

## Use Trove When You Need

- A personal search engine across all your content (GitHub, local files, screenshots, videos)
- An MCP server that lets Claude Code access your indexed files via natural language
- A plugin system to connect any content source (Notion, Obsidian, Figma, Discord...)
- Local-first semantic search without sending data to the cloud
- A CLI tool to index and search content from the terminal
- A cyberpunk web dashboard to browse and find content visually

## Quick Start

```bash
npx trove-os init          # Create config
npx trove-os index         # Index your content
npx trove-os search "q"    # Search
```

## MCP Integration (Claude Code)

```bash
claude mcp add trove -- npx trove-os mcp
```

Then ask Claude: "Find my terrain screenshots from last week" — Trove handles the search.

## Links

- Repository: https://github.com/antoinebecker10-afk/trove
- License: MIT
- Language: TypeScript
- Package: `npx trove-os`

## Tech Stack

TypeScript monorepo (pnpm + Turborepo), React (Vite), MCP SDK (@modelcontextprotocol/sdk), Zod validation, Node.js 20+.

## Connector Plugin System

Anyone can write a connector: implement the `Connector` interface, export it, publish as `trove-connector-{name}`. ~50 lines of code for a basic connector.

## Keywords

personal knowledge management, semantic file search, MCP server, content indexing, developer tools, CLI, local-first, self-hosted, creator tools, AI-native, Claude Code, Model Context Protocol, plugin architecture, second brain, file finder
